description: Per-model hallucination and drift patterns with security-domain-specific mitigations. Dual-provider: Gemini + OpenAI.

tags.OBS: Direct retrieval from verified source (NVD, MITRE, vendor docs)
tags.DRV: Inferred from observed data using documented methodology
tags.GEN: Widely known security principle or standard
tags.STALE: Data older than 90 days without revalidation
tags.CONFLICT: Multiple sources disagree, both values listed

security_validation_rules.message: CVE number does not match expected format. Gated.
CVSS score outside valid range. Gated.
Non-standard severity. Normalized to closest match.
Compliance control ID format not validated. Tagged [SPEC].

strengths: Good at maintaining logical consistency across long outputs

known_drift_patterns.description: May expand threat model scope beyond defined boundaries when finding interesting attack paths
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Enforce scope boundary check after each STRIDE category completion

known_drift_patterns.description: Generates plausible CVE numbers that may not exist in NVD
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Tag all CVE references as [SPEC] unless directly retrieved from NVD API

known_drift_patterns.description: Reports specific CVSS scores (e.g., 7.8) for vulnerabilities without citing source
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Tag CVSS as [SPEC] unless source URL provided. Use ranges instead (high: 7.0-8.9)

audhd_considerations.context_window_usage: Excellent for map-over-steps. Can hold full system architecture.
audhd_considerations.monotropism_alignment: Good at deep single-thread analysis. May need explicit scope reminders.

strengths: Strong at cross-referencing visual and text evidence
Good structured output for decision matrices

known_drift_patterns.description: May identify security issues in diagram elements that are ambiguous or decorative
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Require explicit 'I see [element]' before 'This is vulnerable to [threat]'

known_drift_patterns.description: May assume component functionality from label text that could be misleading
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Tag diagram-derived findings as [DRV] and request confirmation

audhd_considerations.context_window_usage: Good for visual analysis but may require separate text follow-up.
audhd_considerations.externalization_support: Generates tables from visual input effectively.
audhd_considerations.monotropism_alignment: Use for visual-first tasks only. Don't mix visual and text-only analysis.

known_drift_patterns.description: Under speed pressure, may compress SEV2 to SEV3 or miss escalation criteria
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Include explicit escalation checklist in every triage output

known_drift_patterns.description: May default to generic 'patch and monitor' advice instead of specific actions
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Require specific remediation: tool, command, config change, or link

known_drift_patterns.description: May miss threat interactions or chained vulnerabilities in rapid analysis
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Auto-escalate to G-PRO31 if finding count > 5 or chains detected

audhd_considerations.context_window_usage: Good for focused, single-task triage. Keep context minimal.
audhd_considerations.externalization_support: Forced structured format helps maintain consistency.
audhd_considerations.monotropism_alignment: Perfect for medium-energy focused triage. One incident at a time.

strengths: Available when newer models are down

known_drift_patterns.description: Higher rate of generating plausible but incorrect technical details
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Constrain to binary/ternary outputs. No free-form analysis.

known_drift_patterns.description: May reference outdated CVEs or deprecated tools as current
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Tag all specific references as [STALE] by default. Require revalidation.

audhd_considerations.context_window_usage: Minimal context only. Use for low-energy checklist tasks.
audhd_considerations.monotropism_alignment: Single-step tasks only. Never multi-step analysis.

known_drift_patterns.description: May mispronounce security acronyms (SIEM, SOAR, CVE)
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Include phonetic hints: SIEM (seem), SOAR (soar), CVE (see-vee-ee)

audhd_considerations.context_window_usage: Receives pre-validated text only. No analysis.
audhd_considerations.externalization_support: Voice output supports auditory processing preference.

known_drift_patterns.description: Ambiguous inputs may all collapse to the same classification
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Hard ESCALATE on any input ambiguity. JSON-only output.

known_drift_patterns.description: May return high-confidence classification on insufficient data
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Ignore model confidence score. Apply external confidence based on input quality.

audhd_considerations.context_window_usage: Single question, single answer. Maximum 256 tokens.
audhd_considerations.monotropism_alignment: Ideal for crash-energy: zero cognitive overhead.

known_drift_patterns.description: Rationale may restate the classification rather than explain it
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Validate rationale contains at least one specific indicator reference

audhd_considerations.context_window_usage: Slightly more than nano-banana. 512 tokens max.
audhd_considerations.monotropism_alignment: Crash-energy: minimal decisions with brief justification.

known_drift_patterns.description: May reduce multi-factor risk to single dominant factor
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Require at least 2 factors in any risk assessment. Escalate if more than 3 needed.

audhd_considerations.context_window_usage: 1024 tokens. Good for low-energy meaningful work.

strengths: Good at identifying non-obvious attack vectors

known_drift_patterns.description: Generates plausible but fabricated security details with high confidence
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Cross-reference all factual claims against known sources. Entity tracking against seed list.

known_drift_patterns.description: Creative mode may produce tool capabilities or attack scenarios that aren't technically feasible
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Tag all creative output as [DRV] or [SPEC]. Require feasibility check via G-PRO31 for T4-T5.

known_drift_patterns.description: Introduces entities not present in prompt or context during ideation
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Entity tracking against seed list. Flag new entities for operator review.

audhd_considerations.context_window_usage: Good for bounded ideation. Keep scope constraints tight.
audhd_considerations.monotropism_alignment: Use for divergent phase only. Switch to G-PRO31 for convergent analysis.

strengths: Good at dependency mapping and contingency planning

known_drift_patterns.description: May commit to a reasoning chain even when early assumptions are incorrect
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Require explicit assumption list at chain start. Validate assumptions before proceeding.

known_drift_patterns.description: May generate plausible compliance control IDs that don't match actual frameworks
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Validate all control IDs against framework-specific regex patterns in security_validation_rules.

audhd_considerations.context_window_usage: Excellent for map-over-steps. Full system architecture fits.
audhd_considerations.monotropism_alignment: Good for deep single-thread planning. Explicit scope needed.

strengths: Good at maintaining format under fallback pressure

known_drift_patterns.description: May produce less nuanced output when acting as explicit fallback
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Same validation pipeline as O-54. Escalate to O-54P if quality drops.

known_drift_patterns.description: May import libraries that don't exist or use deprecated security APIs
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: SK-CODEREVIEW validates all imports. Cross-check against requirements.txt/pyproject.toml.

known_drift_patterns.description: Generated tests may assert surface behavior without testing actual security fix
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Require negative test cases (attack still blocked) alongside positive cases.

audhd_considerations.context_window_usage: Good for focused code tasks. One file/function at a time.
audhd_considerations.externalization_support: Code is inherently structured. Pair with finding table.
audhd_considerations.monotropism_alignment: Perfect for medium-energy code sprints. Single-file focus.

known_drift_patterns.description: May VERIFY claims that match common security patterns even if specific claim is wrong
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Never use as sole verifier for T4-T5. Cross-validate critical claims.

known_drift_patterns.description: Cannot handle multi-step verification. May return VERIFIED on surface match.
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Hard ESCALATE if verification requires more than 1 reasoning step.

audhd_considerations.context_window_usage: Minimal context. Single claim, single check.
